applied_optimizations.name: GEMM config tuning for small M (M_LEQ_4/8/16)
applied_optimizations.description: Added M_LEQ_4, M_LEQ_8, M_LEQ_16 configs with small BLOCK_SIZE_M (4/8/16) optimized for batch=1 decode workload

applied_optimizations.name: Disable GLU path in pa_mqa_logits
applied_optimizations.description: Changed enable_gluon_pa_mqa_logits condition to False to avoid suboptimal code path

applied_optimizations.name: Fix config dict mutation in fused_gemm
applied_optimizations.description: Added config = dict(config) to prevent config mutation issues

applied_optimizations.name: GEMM NUM_KSPLIT=4 for BF16 atomic GEMM
applied_optimizations.description: Enabled NUM_KSPLIT=4 for M_LEQ_4 config to parallelize K-dimension computation, reducing 36.9ms to 31.0ms

attempted_but_failed.name: PYTORCH_TUNABLEOP
attempted_but_failed.reason: No improvement - GEMM configs already well-tuned by existing backends

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile
attempted_but_failed.reason: Graph breaks in model code (NotImplementedError in multi_platform.py forward_native) and CustomAllreduce cleanup issues

attempted_but_failed.name: NSA decode backend switch (aiter)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incompatible - no heuristic kernel for bf16 GQA=8 configuration

attempted_but_failed.name: MoE block_m tuning (16, 64)
attempted_but_failed.reason: block_m=16 gave 40.0ms, block_m=64 caused crash - default 32 is optimal

attempted_but_failed.name: Aggressive GEMM waves_per_eu=8
attempted_but_failed.reason: Increased latency to 61.7ms - too aggressive for small M decode

next_moves: 1. Try NUM_KSPLIT tuning for FP8 GEMM (gfx950-GEMM-A8W8_BLOCKSCALE.json) - may yield additional 2-3ms improvement
2. Profile to confirm GEMM is still the bottleneck after NUM_KSPLIT=4 optimization

4. Explore MoE 2-stage mode with tuned parameters for batch=1
notes: Achieved 31.0ms decode median latency (target was <=32.55ms) - 28.6% improvement from 43.4ms baseline
Key optimizations: (1) M_LEQ_* GEMM configs for small batch decode, (2) NUM_KSPLIT=4 for BF16 atomic GEMM
NUM_KSPLIT=2 gave 33.1ms, NUM_KSPLIT=4 gave 31.0ms - diminishing returns but still beneficial
MoE block_m tuning (16, 64) did not help - default 32 is optimal for this workload
Aggressive waves_per_eu=8 made performance worse (61.7ms) - conservative tuning is better for small M
Active backends: tilelang (NSA attention), aiter (MoE, all-reduce, RoPE), deepgemm (FP8 GEMM)
Files modified in /sgl-workspace/aiter/ and /sgl-workspace/sglang/ are the runtime locations

applied_optimizations.name: FP8 GEMM NUM_KSPLIT tuning for small M
applied_optimizations.description: Set NUM_KSPLIT=8 for M_LEQ_4, NUM_KSPLIT=4 for M_LEQ_8, NUM_KSPLIT=2 for M_LEQ_16 in FP8 GEMM config to parallelize K-dimension computation, reducing 31.0ms to 22.3ms

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_7
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 7 was killed (exit 137) again after 23 seconds. The agent is spending too much time on file reads and reasoning before executing anything. The FP8 GEMM NUM_KSPLIT tuning has never been successfu

notes: Achieved 22.3ms decode median latency - 48.6% improvement from 43.4ms baseline, 28% improvement from 31.0ms stage1 best
Key optimization: FP8 GEMM NUM_KSPLIT tuning (8/4/2 for M_LEQ_4/8/16) - K-dimension parallelization very effective
Combined with stage1 BF16 GEMM NUM_KSPLIT=4, both GEMM types now optimized for small batch decode
Active backends unchanged: tilelang (NSA), aiter (MoE, all-reduce, RoPE), deepgemm (FP8 GEMM)
Next stage should profile to identify new bottleneck distribution
Hit plateau at 22.3ms after extensive tuning: BF16 NUM_KSPLIT=8, MoE 2-stage, waves_per_eu=4, FP8 NUM_KSPLIT=16 all gave no improvement
Profiling with CUDA graphs enabled shows limited kernel visibility - kernels encapsulated in graph
Target 20.5ms requires ~8% improvement; would need breakthrough in all-reduce (6%), MoE (8%), or elementwise (10%) optimization
torch.compile appears active (TorchDynamo/AOTDispatcher seen in profile) but graph breaks may limit effectiveness

global_notes: FINAL RESULT: 22.3ms decode median latency achieved (48.6% improvement from 43.4ms baseline)
Target 20.5ms NOT met - gap of 1.8ms (8.8%) remains after extensive optimization
Major optimizations applied: (1) GEMM NUM_KSPLIT tuning for FP8 (8/4/2) and BF16 (4), (2) M_LEQ_* configs for small batch, (3) Disable GLU path in pa_mqa_logits
Optimization plateau reached at 22.3ms - multiple attempts (BF16 NUM_KSPLIT=8, MoE 2-stage, waves_per_eu=4, FP8 NUM_KSPLIT=16) yielded no improvement
Kernel breakdown at 22.3ms: FP8 GEMM ~32%, BF16 GEMM ~27%, elementwise/memory ~10%, MoE ~8%, all-reduce ~6%, attention ~4.6%
Attention backend sweep (tilelang, flashmla, aiter, triton) - all resulted in 22.3ms
torch.compile attempted but failed due to NotImplementedError in multi_platform.py forward_native (NSA indexer lacks native implementation)
Profiling without CUDA graphs crashes with GPU memory access faults - limited kernel visibility
Remaining optimization avenues exhausted: all-reduce (max ~0.65ms savings even with 50% improvement), MoE (2-stage mode no improvement), elementwise (would require significant source-level fusion)
Conclusion: 22.3ms appears to be practical limit with current optimization approaches on MI355X TP=8 for batch=1 decode workload
